meta_title: Roofing in Margate Estates, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Expert roofing in Margate Estates, FL. Hurricane-rated shingles & tile from CertainTeed & West Lake. Licensed, local, A+ BBB. Free estimates—call Premier Impact Windows & Roofing.
h1: Trusted Roofing Solutions for Margate Estates Homeowners Built to Handle South Florida's Worst
intro:
Margate Estates is a tight-knit residential community tucked within Broward County's 33063 ZIP code, where tree-lined streets, well-kept single-family homes, and a strong sense of neighborhood pride define the character of everyday life. Near the green open spaces of Margate Estates Park, families have built real roots here—and with average home values hovering around $320,000, that investment is worth protecting from the ground up. For most homeowners in this area, the roof is the single most important line of defense against the storms that sweep through South Florida season after season. When that roof is compromised, everything underneath it is at risk.

Nearly half of all homes in Margate Estates were built before 1994—meaning they predate the sweeping revisions to Florida's construction standards that came in the aftermath of Hurricane Andrew. Older roofing systems installed under previous building codes may not be rated to handle the 170 mph wind zone designation that applies to this area of Broward County. Add in decades of Florida's punishing combination of intense UV radiation, seasonal torrential rain, and annual hurricane threats, and it becomes clear why roofing upgrades and full replacements are one of the most common—and most necessary—home improvement projects in the community.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've spent years working with Margate Estates homeowners to replace aging, code-deficient roofs with modern hurricane-rated systems that meet current Florida Building Code standards. Whether your home has a traditional asphalt shingle roof that's cracked, cupped, or simply past its useful life, or a tile system that suffered impact damage during a past storm season, our experienced crews deliver professional full roof replacements and targeted repairs using premium products from CertainTeed and West Lake. As the Kings of Service, we bring the craftsmanship, the compliance expertise, and the local knowledge to give every Margate Estates home the roof it deserves.

section_why.h2: Why Margate Estates Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Roofing Projects
section_why.body:
When you hire a roofing contractor in Margate Estates, you're not just buying materials—you're buying the expertise, accountability, and workmanship that determine whether your roof lasts 10 years or 30. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing stands apart in Broward County because every installation is handled entirely by our own in-house crews. We don't subcontract your project to third-party laborers or rotating teams. When we show up on your property, those are our employees—background-checked, experienced professionals who are invested in the quality of every nail, every membrane layer, and every ridge cap we install.

With more than 60 years of combined experience across our installation team and over 500 homes completed throughout Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, we understand the specific demands that South Florida's climate places on roofing systems. Margate Estates may sit roughly 8 miles from the coast, but that distance does not insulate the community from serious hurricane exposure. Inland storm tracks, wind-driven rain, and indirect hurricane force can be just as destructive miles from the shore as they are at the waterfront. Our teams are trained and equipped to install roofing systems that meet the structural performance requirements for this region—not just the minimum, but the best available.

We hold an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau, and we're fully licensed and insured throughout the state of Florida. Every project we take on in Margate Estates is permitted through the Broward County Building Division, inspected by local officials, and documented for your records. That documentation matters when it comes time to file a wind mitigation inspection, apply for insurance discounts, or sell your home. From first contact to final inspection, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing treats your home the way we'd treat our own—with respect, precision, and the kind of follow-through that earns referrals from neighbors on the same block.

section_history.h2: Margate Estates Has Seen the Storms—Here's What That Means for Your Roof
section_history.body:
Margate Estates and the surrounding Broward County communities have faced nine named storms since 1990, and three of those events left lasting marks on the regional housing stock. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 devastated South Florida and served as the catalyst for a complete overhaul of the state's building codes. Homes in Margate Estates that were already built by the time Andrew made landfall—roughly 48% of the current housing stock—were constructed under standards that simply didn't anticipate the scale of damage a major hurricane could cause. Many of those same roofs, if not already replaced, may still be operating with outdated fastening patterns, underlayment systems, and deck attachment methods.

Hurricane Wilma struck in 2005 as one of the most powerful Atlantic hurricanes on record, cutting across Broward County with sustained winds that stripped shingles from older rooftops and exposed decking across entire neighborhoods. The repair work that followed Wilma revealed just how many homes in communities like Margate Estates had roofs that were aging, inadequately fastened, or simply overdue for replacement. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, which tracked directly over much of Broward County and caused widespread roofing damage from both wind uplift and wind-driven rain infiltration.

Each of these storms tells the same story: in South Florida, a roof is never just a finishing detail. It is a structural system that must be built, maintained, and replaced with storm performance as the primary consideration. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing designs every roofing project with that reality at the forefront, ensuring Margate Estates homeowners have a roof system that's prepared for what this region's storm history tells us is inevitable.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements Every Margate Estates Homeowner Should Know
section_code.body:
Margate Estates falls under the jurisdiction of the Broward County Building Division and is subject to the Florida Building Code, 8th Edition. While this community is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ)—a special designation that applies to Miami-Dade and parts of Broward closest to the coast—it still carries a 170 mph wind speed design requirement. That means all roofing materials, fastening systems, underlayment, and deck attachment methods must be rated and installed to withstand those wind loads. This is a meaningful standard, and it's one that not every contractor fully understands or consistently meets.

For homes in the 33063 ZIP code, any full roof replacement requires a permit pulled through the Broward County Building Division. The permit process includes a plan review, inspections at key stages of installation, and a final inspection before the project is considered closed. Skipping this process—or working with a contractor who does—can create serious problems when you go to sell your home, file an insurance claim, or request a wind mitigation inspection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf, from application submission to final approval, so you never have to worry about compliance gaps.

For pre-1994 homes in Margate Estates, code upgrades during a full replacement may be required, including improvements to roof deck attachment using ring-shank nails at reduced spacing, secondary water barriers, and rated roofing products with appropriate Florida Product Approval numbers. Our team is thoroughly familiar with these requirements and ensures every project meets current code from start to finish.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Shingles and West Lake Tile: Premium Roofing Systems Built for Broward County
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two of the most respected roofing product lines available in South Florida: CertainTeed architectural shingles from the Landmark series and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. Both product families are engineered to perform in high-wind environments, carry Florida Product Approval certifications, and are available in a range of styles and colors to complement the diverse architectural character of Margate Estates homes.

CertainTeed's Landmark series is one of the most popular architectural shingle choices in the state for good reason. These dimensional, laminated shingles feature a multi-layer construction that adds both aesthetic depth and structural integrity. They carry a Class 4 impact resistance rating and are wind-rated to meet the demands of Broward County's 170 mph wind zone. The Landmark series also comes with CertainTeed's SureStart Protection, offering full replacement coverage during the early years of ownership. For Margate Estates homeowners with shingle roofs looking for a durable, cost-effective, and visually appealing replacement system, CertainTeed Landmark is a standout choice.

For homes with tile roofing—a common and beloved aesthetic throughout South Florida—West Lake's concrete and clay tile systems offer outstanding durability, wind resistance, and thermal performance. Tile roofs naturally resist UV degradation and can last 40 to 50 years when properly installed and maintained. West Lake products meet Florida's stringent approval requirements and are installed by our crews using code-compliant mortar, adhesive, or mechanical fastening methods as appropriate to each project. Whether your Margate Estates home has an existing tile system in need of replacement or you're making the switch from shingles to tile, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has the product expertise and installation experience to deliver a result you'll be proud of for decades.

section_cost.h2: What Does a New Roof Cost in Margate Estates—And How Can You Afford It?
section_cost.body:
For Margate Estates homeowners with properties averaging $320,000 in value, a quality roof replacement is one of the smartest investments you can make in your home's long-term performance and marketability. The cost of a full roof replacement in this area typically ranges from $12,000 to $22,000 depending on the size of the home, the roofing material selected, the condition of the existing deck, and whether any structural repairs are needed as part of the project. CertainTeed architectural shingles tend to fall toward the lower to mid range of this spectrum, while West Lake tile systems represent a premium investment with exceptional longevity.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing understands that a project of this scale can feel daunting for middle-income households, which is why we offer flexible financing options designed to make quality roofing accessible. Our financing programs start as low as $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners. Spreading the cost of your roof replacement over manageable monthly payments allows you to act now rather than delaying while storm season approaches—and waiting often costs more in the form of accelerating damage, water intrusion, and emergency repairs.

The return on investment for a new roof in Margate Estates is compelling. A properly documented, permitted, and code-compliant roof replacement can increase resale value, reduce homeowner's insurance premiums through wind mitigation credits, and eliminate the ongoing costs of patching and repairing a system that's reached the end of its useful life. When you factor in the insurance savings that many Broward County homeowners see after a wind mitigation inspection—sometimes hundreds of dollars annually—the true net cost of a new roof is often significantly lower than the sticker price suggests.

section_insurance.h2: How a New Roof in Margate Estates Can Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance Premiums
section_insurance.body:
Homeowner's insurance in Broward County has become one of the most significant ongoing costs for Florida homeowners, and Margate Estates residents are no exception. Insurers in this market price policies based heavily on the age and condition of the roof, the type of roofing material, and the results of a wind mitigation inspection. A roof that's over 15 years old, installed without secondary water barriers, or fastened to the deck with smooth-shank nails can significantly inflate your annual premium—sometimes by thousands of dollars compared to a home with a modern, code-compliant roof system.

When you replace your roof with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing using CertainTeed or West Lake systems and meet current Florida Building Code requirements, you become eligible for a wind mitigation inspection conducted by a licensed inspector. The OIR-B1-1802 Wind Mitigation Form—Florida's standard document used by insurers—rates your roof on multiple characteristics including deck attachment, roof covering type, roof-to-wall connections, and the presence of a secondary water barrier. Scoring well on this form can yield substantial discounts across multiple rating categories with carriers like Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, Heritage Insurance, and others serving the Broward County market.

Many Margate Estates homeowners who complete a full roof replacement with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing report annual insurance savings ranging from $400 to over $1,500 depending on their carrier, policy structure, and the specific wind mitigation credits they qualify for. Over the life of a 30-year roof, those savings can add up to tens of thousands of dollars—making a new roof not just a safety upgrade but a financially prudent decision. Our team is happy to walk you through which features of your new roofing system will have the greatest impact on your wind mitigation score.

faqs.q: My Margate Estates home was built in the late 1980s. Does that mean my roof needs to be fully replaced?
faqs.a: Not necessarily, but it's worth a thorough inspection. Homes built before 1994 in Margate Estates were constructed under pre-Andrew building codes and may have roofing systems that don't meet current 170 mph wind zone requirements. During a full replacement, code-compliant upgrades to deck fastening, underlayment, and roof covering are required. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers free evaluations to assess whether your roof needs a full replacement or targeted repairs.

faqs.q: How much will a roof replacement cost for my home in Margate Estates, and do you offer financing?
faqs.a: Most full roof replacements for homes in the Margate Estates area range from $12,000 to $22,000 depending on square footage, material selection, and deck conditions. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified homeowners. We provide detailed, itemized estimates upfront so you know exactly what you're paying for before any work begins.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my roof in Margate Estates, and does Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handle that?
faqs.a: Yes, a permit is required for any full roof replacement in Margate Estates through the Broward County Building Division. All work must be inspected and meet Florida Building Code 8th Edition requirements, including wind-rated materials and proper installation methods. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permit process on your behalf—from application to final inspection—so you remain fully compliant without having to navigate the permitting system yourself.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take for a typical home in Margate Estates?
faqs.a: Most single-family home roof replacements in Margate Estates are completed in one to three days for shingle systems, while tile installations may take three to five days depending on the size and complexity of the roof. The permitting process may add one to two weeks prior to the start of work. Our project managers communicate the full timeline before work begins and keep you updated throughout the process so there are no surprises.

faqs.q: Will a new roof really lower my homeowner's insurance premiums in Broward County?
faqs.a: Yes, in most cases it will. A new roof that meets current Florida Building Code requirements in Margate Estates makes you eligible for a wind mitigation inspection. The results of that inspection—evaluated using Florida's OIR-B1-1802 form—can qualify you for significant discounts across multiple rating categories with most carriers serving Broward County. Many homeowners see annual savings between $400 and $1,500 or more, helping offset the cost of the roof replacement over time.

cta_headline: Margate Estates Homeowners: Get a Free Roof Estimate from the Kings of Service
cta_body: Don't wait for the next storm to find out your roof isn't ready—Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is here to help you protect your home with expert installation and hurricane-rated products. Contact us today for your free, no-obligation roof evaluation.
